title: My Experiences
text: Over the years I've worked in pretty different environments — NGOs, fintechs, big media companies, US corporations, and freelance. Some roles were about leading teams and owning technical decisions, others were about shipping fast as an individual contributor. The variety pushed me to adapt constantly, and that's probably where I grew the most.
subtitle: My experience as a developer

experiences.text: Rebuilt the entire smart TV streaming app from scratch and shipped a production-ready platform within 6 months. Optimized the UI to run on low-end hardware (2018 Tizen and WebOS models), cutting memory usage by 50% compared to the previous version. Handled full cross-platform support across Tizen, WebOS, Vizio, and Comcast TVs, and wrote comprehensive unit and integration tests to ensure UI stability across all devices.

Developed features and UI components for Globoplay — Globo's streaming platform serving millions of active users across Brazil. Built reusable, performant React components using Canvas Web APIs to handle playback and UI rendering on Samsung, LG, and Sony smart TVs. Tracked down and resolved complex performance bottlenecks and memory leaks in a hardware-constrained environment where any lag is immediately visible to millions of users.

Modernized a 10-year-old microservices codebase — upgrading Node.js 12→24 and MongoDB 4→8 and refactoring it for modern Docker environments. Fixed security flaws across the entire stack, bringing open vulnerabilities from hundreds down to zero. Optimized backend architecture and database indexes, scaling throughput from 10 to over 100 RPS. Migrated version control from AWS CodeArtifact to GitHub and set up automated CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions.

Joined as tech lead at World Pulse (NGO based in Portland, Oregon) and owned the full platform build — from technology selection and AWS infrastructure setup to day-to-day team management. Architected and shipped the entire platform from scratch using NextJS, Node.js, GraphQL, Directus, Postgres, and Strapi. Led sprint planning, all code reviews, and every release cycle across a distributed international team.

Three years building a multi-tenant platform for car dealerships from the ground up. At our peak we had over 150 live sites running on it. This is where I got serious about web performance — every site hit 100% on Google Lighthouse across all categories, and they all ranked on Google's first page. I handled the full stack: Laravel and MariaDB on the backend, Node and Express on the API layer, and all the designs too.

Freelancing taught me how to adapt fast. I've built everything from landing pages to full applications, usually working independently across the full stack. It's where I learned to ship things quickly, make pragmatic decisions, and communicate clearly with non-technical clients.

In 2019 my team built Bari's banking website using NextJS — one of the very first projects we'd done with it. Back then the framework was still niche and the community was tiny. It was an early bet that paid off.
